howItWorks.intro: This pizza dough hydration calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Flour = Total Dough x (100 / (100 + Hydration% + Salt% + Yeast%))
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What hydration should pizza dough be?
faq.answer: Neapolitan: 60-65%. New York style: 63-67%. Thin and crispy: 55-60%. Sicilian/pan: 70-80%. Higher hydration gives a lighter, more open crumb but is harder to handle. Start at 63% and adjust.

faq.question: What is baker's percentage?
faq.answer: Baker's percentage expresses each ingredient as a percentage of the flour weight. Flour is always 100%. So 65% hydration means 650g water for every 1000g flour. This makes scaling recipes easy.

howWeCalculate.intro: This pizza dough hydration calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.

Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
